title: dactylorhiza × braunii nothosubsp. monticola
intro: Dactylorhiza × braunii nothosubsp. monticola, also known as the Braun's Spotted Orchid, is a hybrid plant species resulting from a cross between Dactylorhiza maculata and Dactylorhiza sambucina. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other compounds. The Braun's Spotted Orchid has been used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its ability to relax the airways and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used as an infusion, or in the form of capsules or tinctures, to harness its medicinal benefits and promote overall well-being.
